plugins.//: Stay in 0.x while the API settles: a breaking change bumps the minor, not the major. The response models were observed against a single account, so unusual states (frozen cards, other currencies, send/withdrawal rows) are still unverified and will move. The BREAKING CHANGE notes still appear in the release notes.

Bump package.json only. The publish is done by the workflow with npm's trusted publishing (OIDC): @semantic-release/npm hard-requires an NPM_TOKEN (set-npmrc-auth throws ENONPMTOKEN without one) and has no OIDC support, and a long-lived, 2FA-bypassing token in CI is exactly the npm supply-chain vector we are avoiding. With npmPublish:false it skips the auth check entirely.
